evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 10 Questions but page shows ~19 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Post is 1593 words (pillar) — include a <nav aria-label="Table of contents"> for LLM outline extraction
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Has a voice in flashes ('herding cats') but is mostly editorial boilerplate; a stray editing instruction literally made it into the post.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 5/10 — Some opinions surface (rentals beat hotels, skip certain things) but most claims are generic travel-guide consensus.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — A few decent lines (herding cats, hangry mutinies, plate lunch for the plane), but surrounded by predictable travel-blog phrasing.
Point of view vs both-sides survey 5/10 — Tends to list options evenhandedly rather than commit to opinions.
Quotable sentences 5/10 — The group-chat opening and plate-lunch closer are quotable; most of the body isn't.
Intro earns attention 3/10 — Buried under metadata, TL;DR, bylines, and a 'Quick answer' before the actual hook lands.
